canonical_question: How do I design a URL shortener at scale?
aliases: How to build a URL shortening service like Bitly or TinyURL?
Design a URL shortener with billions of links
constraints: Short codes must be at least 6-7 characters in base62 to support billions of unique URLs (62^7 = 3.5 trillion)
Read-to-write ratio is ~100:1; optimize the redirect (read) path first
Never use HTTP 301 (permanent redirect) if you need click analytics
URL validation and malware scanning must happen at write time, not redirect time
Cache invalidation must propagate to all edge nodes when a short URL is deleted or updated

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Need to design a paste/content-sharing service (Pastebin)
Need a CDN or content delivery architecture

inputs_needed.question: What is the expected scale? (URLs created per day and redirects per day)
Do you need click analytics (geo, referrer, device)?
Do you need custom/vanity short URLs?
